title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Retrieval Circuit

Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for authorized TypeScript discovery policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted, parsed, resolved, redirected, selected, and rejected URL evidence with tenant, actor, policy, and bounded causes.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Separate evidence from confidence · Review Feed Ts Discovery Audit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: The stored final URL fully explains source admission.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Discovery Audit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Retain submitted, parsed, resolved, redirected, selected, and rejected URL evidence with tenant, actor, policy, and bounded causes.
